title: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume Non Anime
display_title: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume Non Anime
wikitext:
[]: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by asakuraikun » Tue Sep 12, 2023 11:07 pm I'd like to know things that Evangelion led you to consume, but other than anime/manga. I know many people who gave a chance to mecha anime (or even anime in general) after watching Evangelion. I know many people may share this story as well. BUT what I'd like to hear is about other media Evangelion induced you to consume - books, movies, philosophy books, psychology books, religious books, even other kinds of Japanese media, etc. Share if you can, maybe someone can even pick something from here too. Thanks!

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Sep 13, 2023 8:56 am That's a great question! I started listening to French house because it reminded me of the general style of Evangelion (late 90s, futuristic aesthetic). A friend of mine here on the site also helped me get hooked by sharing some of his favorite songs with me. The same goes for classical music. It also inspired me to start looking for new types of films (things outside of the usual blockbuster releases). Some of my favorite movies today have an Evangelion touch to them (American Beauty, The Shining, After Sun, Cowboy Bebop: The Movie, etc).

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Wed Sep 13, 2023 8:06 pm It was only one of many, many reasons, but Evangelion is partly why I read the entire King James Bible (Plus a fair amount of the "apocryphal" books, albeit in NRSV translation) last year.

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by asakuraikun » Wed Sep 13, 2023 9:59 pm BernardoCairo wrote: That's a great question! I started listening to French house because it reminded me of the general style of Evangelion (late 90s, futuristic aesthetic). A friend of mine here on the site also helped me get hooked by sharing some of his favorite songs with me. The same goes for classical music. It also inspired me to start looking for new types of films (things outside of the usual blockbuster releases). Some of my favorite movies today have an Evangelion touch to them (American Beauty, The Shining, After Sun, Cowboy Bebop: The Movie, etc).

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Wed Sep 13, 2023 10:06 pm Believe me it certainly was an effort! I'm not religious at all but reading the Bible helped fill in a lot of holes in Western culture for me, and was pretty fascinating in its own right. Haven't heard of Gospel of Barnabas before but it sounds interesting (Though I'm guessing I'd get way more out of it after studying more about Islam). The only non-canonical Gospel I looked at was the Infancy Gospel of Thomas which has kinda funny stories about Jesus as a young boy- curiously though, it seems like some of its stories worked their way into the Quran somehow.

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu Sep 21, 2023 6:53 am asakuraikun wrote: About French House, any recommendations?

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by asakuraikun » Sat Oct 21, 2023 11:31 pm The Killer of Heroes wrote: Haven't heard of Gospel of Barnabas before but it sounds interesting (Though I'm guessing I'd get way more out of it after studying more about Islam). The only non-canonical Gospel I looked at was the Infancy Gospel of Thomas which has kinda funny stories about Jesus as a young boy- curiously though, it seems like some of its stories worked their way into the Quran somehow.

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Fri Nov 10, 2023 9:16 pm asakuraikun wrote: the mentioned Sonic CD soundtrack mentioned is one of my favourite soundtracks out of a 16-bit machine

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by angelofgendo » Thu Nov 16, 2023 3:30 am I'm curious to read the psychology book Anno read while developing NGE. And, Miss Misato had inspired me to consume Yebisu and steak dinners.

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by ::KL7:: » Sat Nov 18, 2023 1:52 am I bought a book of Harlan Ellison…mainly because I read about it somewhere here in the forums.

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by LucAAAs » Wed Nov 22, 2023 8:45 pm Evangelion overall lead me to learn more about different cultures and mythologies, which I find really cool as a culture nerd and specially as an artist that enjoys incorporating different philosophies into my drawings and ideas. As much as I appreciate this newfound interest, the thing that most benefitted me certainly was the interest I developed in psychoanalysis, which eventually lead me to discovering how past traumatic experiences were damaging my current self and are guiding me towards healing.

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Nov 29, 2023 3:04 pm Sayonara Jupiter was pretty sweet. I just need to get around to watching UFO at some point.

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by MissDeamy » Thu Dec 07, 2023 11:58 pm Drawing, I mean it inspired me to draw better or at least become crazy with my artwork because back then I would just draw characters standing and having no pose at all but after watching Evangelion it legit inspired me to be more dynamic with my poses when I draw, and so I practice even more now thanks to Evangelion. It also made me wanna draw giant robots, oh the anatomy is so fun to look at haha! Robot are cool

[]: Re: Best Things That Evangelion Inspired You to Consume (Non-Anime)             Quote Post by asakuraikun » Sun Jun 30, 2024 9:02 pm jeffythedragonslayer wrote: I'm curious to read the psychology book Anno read while developing NGE. And, Miss Misato had inspired me to consume Yebisu and steak dinners.
